title: fix: align bounty submission scripts with ETH prepay
body:
## Summary
- update the onboarding script ABI names to the current ETH prepay flow (ethMaxBudget, payable startPreparedSubmission)
- stop treating the prepared ETH budget as a LINK approval amount in submit_to_bounty.js
- add on-chain diagnostics for stuck prepared submissions, including eval wallet code, balance, status, and ETH budget

## Testing
- node --check for every skills/verdikta-bounties-onboarding/scripts/*.js
- node skills/verdikta-bounties-onboarding/scripts/validate_contract_config.js

Closes #17

title: startPreparedSubmission reverts repeatedly for prepared submissions until a fresh prepare succeeds (Base Sepolia, directed bounty)
body:
Observed on Base Sepolia (staging escrow `0xAA67686Bb09F569C2C3b663BB3679dD9f9F60BDC`) during a directed-bounty test on 2026-07-10, driven by a verdikta-agents runtime agent (CDP smart-account hunter).

**Symptom:** on bounty **23** (a `targetHunter`-directed bounty), four consecutive submission lifecycles each prepared successfully on-chain but every `startPreparedSubmission` user operation **reverted on-chain**:

- submissions 0–3, prepared 03:39:42 → 03:45:34 UTC, all stuck `Prepared` (status 0)
- the caller attached the **exact** `ethMaxBudget` from each submission's own `SubmissionPrepared` event (240000000000000 wei — verified against `getSubmission`), so this is not the `msg.value` equality check
- hunter = the bounty's `targetHunter` (`0xC355b4Fc77b53F6716211ef054Ea72A8d57fc6A7`), so it is not the target restriction
- wallet held ~0.0043 ETH, far above budget + gas
- example failed user-op hashes: `0xc14093167d42b1c6197280f385d981289b6864199e7745ef894196405c89aa37`, `0x35a98d6dd5d4bc8af7ee1b08e1d14e872eb9fb3a6cc630d9bcbac0fae457846c`, `0x9313d2d5f306038539ba336141ffad6cd5e464684dd6c30daeaa93dbcab81a75`

**The fifth attempt succeeded end-to-end** (submission 4, prepared 03:47:54, started + evaluated 87/100 + finalized + paid within ~3 minutes).

**Observable difference:** submissions 0–3 all carried the same evaluation wallet `0x856AE479E90029C9Ed8fAb145357eb98D47b159A`; the succeeding submission 4 got a different one, `0x9bEa09d534D727cD28e025563Fa504C988a185d8`. Hypothesis (unproven): prepare handed out an evaluation wallet in a state that makes `start` revert (e.g. not yet provisioned/funded on-chain), and re-preparing eventually rotates to a healthy one.

For contrast, the same agent + same code path started bounty **22** (non-directed) successfully on the FIRST attempt ~10 minutes earlier, with evalWallet `0x856AE479…` — so the wallet may have gone stale between the two bounties rather than being directed-bounty-specific.

`diagnoseSubmission` reported the stuck submissions as valid-and-startable ("issues_found: 1 — call startPreparedSubmission"), so the platform's own diagnostics couldn't see the revert cause either.

Happy to provide more transcript detail if useful.

title: Draft rubric validator does not check jury nodes (accepts runs: 0 and dotted model ids)
body:
## Observed

`POST /api/jobs/rubric/validate` validates rubric shape well (must/weight rules, weight sums) but performs **no jury-node validation**. Confirmed two ways on 2026-07-09 (Base Sepolia instance, and earlier on mainnet):

- A jury node with **`runs: 0`** → `valid: true, errors: []`
- A model id in **dot notation** (`claude-sonnet-4.6`, which fails at jury runtime with model-not-found — the hyphenated `claude-sonnet-4-6` is what runs) → `valid: true, errors: []`
- (Also from our earlier probing: a node with no `weight` is accepted — that one appears intentional since it defaults server-side.)

Meanwhile the **create** endpoint (`/api/jobs/create`) 400s on a missing/zero `runs` ("Jury node N: Runs must be a number >= 1") — so the validator promises less than create enforces, and callers who "validate first" (as the agent guide recommends) still get surprised at create time. The dotted-model-id case is worse: **neither** validate nor create catches it, and it only fails at evaluation time inside the jury run (observed live on mainnet bounty #107: the claude-sonnet-4.6 node errored model-not-found and the jury proceeded on the remaining node).

Amusingly, this gap was independently discovered by one of our agents running a shadow-mode QA of its own "validate before create" skill — it deliberately passed malformed jury nodes to the validator and reported "the API does NOT enforce jury-node rules; the agent must self-enforce."

## Suggestion

Bring the draft validator to parity with (or beyond) create-time enforcement:
- `runs` present, integer, ≥ 1 (matches create's rule)
- model ids checked against the supported-model list for the class (ties into #15 — the availability data needs a refresh, but even a syntax/known-id check would catch the dot-notation failure mode before evaluation time)

## Repro

```bash
curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-Bot-API-Key: <key>" \
  -d '{"rubricJson":{"criteria":[{"id":"a","must":true,"weight":0,"description":"gate"},{"id":"b","must":false,"weight":1,"description":"scored"}]},"juryNodes":[{"provider":"anthropic","model":"claude-sonnet-4.6","runs":0,"weight":1}],"classId":128,"workProductType":"writing"}' \
  https://bounties.verdikta.org/api/jobs/rubric/validate
# → {"valid": true, "errors": []}
```

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Model-availability check flags jury models that demonstrably run and score (class 128: gpt-5.4, claude-sonnet-4-6)
body:
## Observed

`GET /api/jobs/108/validate` (Base mainnet, 2026-07-05) returns `valid: false` including these **warning**-severity issues:

```json
{ "type": "MODEL_UNAVAILABLE", "severity": "warning",
  "message": "Jury model openai/gpt-5.4 may not be available in class 128" },
{ "type": "MODEL_UNAVAILABLE", "severity": "warning",
  "message": "Jury model anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 may not be available in class 128" }
```

But that exact jury (`gpt-5.4` 0.5 + `claude-sonnet-4-6` 0.5, runs 1) **demonstrably worked on this very bounty**: it evaluated three submissions on 2026-07-04/05 (scored 46 → REJECTED under the 70 threshold, then 90 → APPROVED) and the winner was paid. Bounty #107's jury also ran fine on `gpt-5.4` while carrying the same warning.

## Impact

- The availability list for class 128 appears stale or over-conservative relative to what the arbiters actually accept.
- Because these are surfaced while a bounty is **OPEN** (and appear to contribute to `valid: false`), open bounties wear a ⚠ Validate chip that reads as "broken" to creators and hunters — see also the companion issue about terminal-state Validate rendering.
- Note the *actual* runtime failure mode is spelled differently: `claude-sonnet-4.6` (dots) fails at jury runtime with model-not-found, while `claude-sonnet-4-6` (hyphens) runs — so the check currently warns on a working id and (shape-validators elsewhere) pass a broken one.

## Suggestion

- Refresh the class-128 supported-model list (or have the check consult the live arbiter model set).
- Consider not folding warning-severity issues into `valid: false` — reserve that for error severity.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Bounty board: Validate shows a red error for every AWARDED bounty (terminal-state semantics)
body:
## Observed

Clicking **Validate** on any bounty in a terminal state returns a red error chip — for **all** AWARDED bounties on the board, regardless of creator or creation path (checked #106, #107, #108 on Base mainnet, 2026-07-05; #106 was created by a different creator via the normal flow).

`GET /api/jobs/106/validate`:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "jobId": 106,
  "valid": false,
  "issues": [
    {
      "type": "CHAIN_STATUS",
      "severity": "error",
      "message": "Bounty is \"AWARDED\" on-chain and cannot accept submissions or pay out."
    }
  ]
}
```

So post-award validation **can never pass by construction** — the check answers "can a hunter submit and get paid right now?", which is legitimately *no* for a completed bounty. But the UI renders that as the same red ⚠ used for genuinely broken bounties, making every **successfully completed** bounty look defective. It cost us real confusion twice while operating the board (we initially read it as an on-chain problem with a live bounty).

## Suggestion

Either:
- hide/disable the Validate button once a bounty reaches a terminal state (AWARDED / CLOSED / EXPIRED), or
- special-case `CHAIN_STATUS` on terminal states to a neutral badge ("Completed — no longer accepting submissions") and reserve the red ⚠ for actionable problems on OPEN bounties.

## Repro

1. Open any AWARDED bounty card on https://bounties.verdikta.org
2. Click **Validate** → red error chip
3. Or: `curl -H "X-Bot-API-Key: <key>" https://bounties.verdikta.org/api/jobs/106/validate`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: example-arbiters: surface arbiter version/status on the dashboard
body:
## Summary

Show each arbiter operator's **software version and upgrade status** on the arbiters dashboard (arbiters.verdikta.org), so fleet operators can see at a glance which nodes are current and which need `upgrade-arbiter.sh`.

## Context / why now

As of `verdikta-arbiter@b9a58e2`, every arbiter embeds a version self-report in each justification it uploads to IPFS:

```json
"arbiter": { "adapter": "1.0.0", "aiNode": "0.1.0", "verdiktaCommon": "1.6.0", "release": "b9a58e2 2026-07-06T21:20:25Z" }
```

The same info is served at `GET /version` on the External Adapter, and `$INSTALL_DIR/VERSION` is stamped by install/upgrade. This was built after a fleet incident where an outdated node (broken Anthropic `top_p` handling) was indistinguishable from healthy nodes on-chain — diagnosing it required manually fetching per-oracle justification CIDs. The `e2e/scripts/audit-oracles.js` CLI in verdikta-arbiter proves the sampling approach works; this issue is about surfacing it in the dashboard UI.

## Data source mechanics (no contract changes needed)

- `FulfillAIEvaluation(aggRequestId, likelihoods, justificationCIDs)` publishes the **clustered oracles' justification CIDs, comma-joined in reveal-record order** (see `_finalizeAggregation` in ReputationAggregator).
- The dashboard's `verdiktaService.getOracleHealth()` event scan already collects these events — it just never fetches the JSONs.
- Justification CIDs are immutable → a write-once file cache (same pattern as the existing `gasReceiptStore`) means each CID is fetched at most once, ever.
- CID → operator attribution: zip the published CIDs against the round's clustered reveal events (verify ordering against BonusPayment/reveal events at implementation time; unattributed samples still count toward fleet version distribution).

## Proposed phases

**Phase 1 — version sampling + Operator Reliability column (core)**
- New server util `versionSampleService` (mirrors `gasReceiptStore`): sample recent fulfills, fetch justification JSONs via IPFS gateway HTTP (Pinata gateway first, `ipfs.io` fallback, capped concurrency/timeouts), extract the `arbiter` block + `model_results`.
- Expose `versionByOp` in `/api/analytics/oracle-health`.
- Client: add a **Version** column to the Operator Reliability table — green badge with release stamp (tooltip: component versions + sample age), gray "not reporting" for pre-versioning nodes.

**Phase 2 — up-to-date status**
- Compare sampled release hash vs latest verdikta-arbiter main (GitHub API, cached; `LATEST_RELEASE` env fallback) → `current / behind / unreported` badges + a Home-page headline ("X of Y operators up-to-date").

**Phase 3 — AggHistory per-oracle breakdown**
- Split the combined justification CID and render a card per revealing oracle: operator, individual scores, version, per-model failures from `model_results`. (Would have diagnosed the top_p incident from the browser.)

**Phase 4 (optional) — MyArbiters badge + Model Health**
- Connected owner sees their operator's sampled version with an "upgrade available" hint; fleet-wide per-model failure-rate rollup (e.g. "Anthropic calls failing 100% on operator X").

## Caveats agreed up front

- **Sampling blind spot**: only operators landing clustered reveals get sampled; idle nodes should show "no recent samples" (long-term complement: on-chain version registration in ReputationKeeper — dispatcher change, out of scope here).
- **README's "no IPFS code" rule**: this adds read-only gateway HTTP fetches only (no pinning, no keys) — the feature set now demands it per the README's own escape clause.

## Effort estimate

Phase 1: ~250–350 lines server + small client change. Phase 2: small. Phase 3: moderate client work.

title: [Skill] Implement bundle submit flow in submit_to_bounty.js
body:
## Summary

Implements the `--bundle` flow in `submit_to_bounty.js` so it fully executes the two-step bundle submission path instead of erroring out.

### What changed
- `--bundle` now calls `POST /submit/bundle`
- signs and broadcasts the returned transaction via the existing signing helper
- calls `POST /submit/bundle/complete` after broadcast
- falls back to receipt log parsing for `submissionId` / `evalWallet` when needed
- keeps the canonical prepare/approve/start path as the default
- updates `SKILL.md` to document the bundle flow

This keeps bundle mode opt-in while making it operational for backends that prefer the bundled submission path.

title: Enforce classMap jury models and remove stale model guidance
body:
Summary

This PR makes @verdikta/common class maps the strict source of truth for allowed jury models, and removes stale/hardcoded model references that can cause deprecated model usage.

Why

We observed deprecated model usage (e.g. claude-3-5-haiku-20241022) still being accepted in some flows due to stale docs/examples and non-blocking validation. This causes stuck evaluations and unreliable agent behavior.


What changed

Backend (authoritative enforcement)

• POST /api/jobs/create now hard-fails if any juryNodes[{provider, model}] is not in classMap.getClass(classId).models.
• Provider normalization added for comparison (OpenAI/openai, etc).
• Clear 400 payload with:
• classId
• invalidNodes
• allowedModels
• /api/jobs/:jobId/validate treats model unavailability as error (not warning) in strict mode.
Class/model API contract

• Added metadata fields to class endpoints:
• classMapVersion
• source: "@verdikta/common"
• generatedAt
Frontend/docs safety

• Removed hardcoded “supported models” text from Agents page.
• Updated text to point to dynamic class/model endpoints.
• Tightened Create Bounty class-switch behavior to avoid silent jury carryover into unverified classes.
Skill/docs updates

• Added explicit preflight requirement: verify every jury model via /api/classes/:classId/models before create.
• Removed deprecated model examples from agent-facing docs/examples.

API behavior changes (breaking)

POST /api/jobs/create now rejects unsupported jury models with HTTP 400.

Example error
{
"error": "Invalid jury configuration",
"details": "One or more jury models are not supported for this class",
"classId": 128,
"allowedModels": ["openai/gpt-5.2-2025-12-11", "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929"],
"invalidNodes": [
{ "provider": "anthropic", "model": "claude-3-5-haiku-20241022" }
]
}
Files touched

• server/routes/jobRoutes.js
• server/utils/bountyValidator.js
• server/server.js
• server/utils/validation.js (if optional class-aware validation path included)
• client/src/pages/Agents.jsx
• client/src/pages/CreateBounty.jsx
• client/src/services/classMapService.js
• skills/verdikta-bounties-onboarding/SKILL.md (or equivalent docs location)
• skills/verdikta-bounties-onboarding/references/api_endpoints.md
• skills/verdikta-bounties-onboarding/references/classes-models-and-agent-api.md

Test plan

Backend tests

• [ ] Create with valid models for class 128 succeeds.
• [ ] Create with deprecated/unsupported model fails with 400 + invalidNodes.
• [ ] /api/jobs/:jobId/validate reports unsupported model as ERROR.
• [ ] /api/classes, /api/classes/:id, /api/classes/:id/models include classMapVersion.
Frontend/manual tests

• [ ] Agents page no longer shows hardcoded stale model names.
• [ ] Create flow blocks invalid jury model/class combos.
• [ ] Class switching does not silently preserve incompatible jury nodes.
Regression checks

• [ ] Existing valid bounty creation flow remains unchanged.
• [ ] Submission/claim flows unaffected for valid bounties.

Rollout notes

• Announce to integrators: create API is now strict on jury model membership.
• Recommend all agents run preflight:
1. GET /api/classes/:classId/models
2. verify juryNodes membership
3. call POST /api/jobs/create

Optional follow-up (separate PR)

Add POST /api/classes/:classId/jury/validate to provide a dedicated preflight endpoint for agents.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Introduces a breaking behavior change by rejecting previously accepted jury configurations at create-time and tightening validation severity; risk is primarily integration/compatibility rather than data or auth.
> 
> **Overview**
> `POST /api/jobs/create` now **strictly validates** `juryNodes` against the active class’s supported models from `@verdikta/common` (with provider name normalization), returning a 400 with `invalidNodes` and `allowedModels` instead of allowing potentially stuck evaluations.
> 
> Class endpoints (`/api/classes`, `/api/classes/:id`, `/api/classes/:id/models`) now include metadata (`classMapVersion`, `source`, `generatedAt`) to support clients caching/diagnostics, and bounty validation escalates class/model mismatches from *warnings* to **errors**.
> 
> Frontend/docs remove hardcoded “supported models” lists and point creators to fetch models dynamically from `/api/classes/:classId/models`, and onboarding examples swap a deprecated Anthropic model for a current one.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 3932fa20931460fa0baca846a32164c3ebaad5dc. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: Serve Vite client as production build via nginx
body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Primarily production deployment/config changes; misconfigured paths, SPA routing, or caching headers could break client serving or cause stale assets in production.
> 
> **Overview**
> Switches production Nginx setup from proxying to Vite dev servers (`localhost:5173/5174`) to serving **static, built** client assets from `/var/www/verdikta-bounties/{base,base-sepolia}`, while continuing to proxy `/api/` to the appropriate backend.
> 
> Adds `client/buildClient.sh` to produce per-network Vite builds and updates the Nginx configs with SPA `try_files` routing and long-lived caching headers for static assets; deployment docs are updated accordingly (build + rsync to web root, only backend services need to run).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit ce029ffd9196661912f92cc76b34ed90a7c3b5d8. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
